sentence_reviews.text: Hidden Markov models generate sequences through latent Markov state, whereas conditional random fields directly model structured output distributions conditioned on observations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML hidden Markov model, state-space/latent-variable sequence model, and conditional random field / structured prediction topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sentence accurately contrasts generative latent-state HMMs and conditional structured CRFs.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Hidden Markov models & CRFs is to connect Sequences, events & time series, Latent variables & hidden state to downstream capabilities including Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection, Document intelligence & information extraction.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML HMM/CRF sequence-model and structured-prediction topics; sequence/time-series and latent-variable chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sequence and latent-state dependencies are supported; document intelligence and anomaly/fraud downstream categories are plausible but not directly established.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
sentence_reviews.notes: HMMs support time-series/sequence modeling; anomaly/fraud is not directly evidenced by the packet source.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Document intelligence & information extraction
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML CRF / structured prediction sequence-labeling topics; no direct document-intelligence passage in packet evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: CRFs are standard for sequence labeling/information extraction, but the broad document-intelligence label is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Sequences, events & time series, Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML HMM/CRF sequence-model topics and model-selection context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are supported, but generic limitation wording is not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: Hidden Markov models & CRFs consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML HMMs and CRFs under sequence models / structured prediction.

sentence_reviews.text: Hidden Markov models & CRFs consumes, emits, or organizes information using Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML HMM latent-state topics; CRF structured output topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Latent hidden state is central for HMMs, but linear-chain CRFs need not contain latent variables. The combined-node claim is only partially supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Hidden Markov models & CRFs is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML HMM inference/learning with EM and dynamic-programming/message-passing; CRF inference topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: EM/message passing are supported for HMMs and graphical sequence models; MCMC is broader than the direct claim.

sentence_reviews.text: Hidden Markov models & CRFs is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML probabilistic sequence model likelihood and CRF conditional likelihood topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Likelihood/conditional likelihood training is standard for HMMs/CRFs; cross-entropy is a related supervised loss framing.

edge_reviews.notes: HMMs and CRFs are sequence/structured-output models; represents_with/core is supported.
Hidden state is core for HMMs but not CRFs generally, so the relationship is only partially supported for the combined node and core strength is too strong.
EM and message-passing/dynamic-programming inference are common for these sequence models; common strength is supported.
Likelihood and conditional-likelihood objectives are standard; optimizes_for/common is supported.
